calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

{"name": "Policy And Legal Advocacy", "source": {"source_type": "web_search", "source_index": null, "page_number": null, "search_result_index": 20, "quote": "In September 2020, WWF-Hong Kong helped create a working group to combat wildlife smuggling. The group was tasked with drafting and leading global consultations which led to the 2021 passage of a new International Maritime Organization global document, Guidelines for the Prevention and Suppression of the Smuggling of Wildlife on Ships Engaged in International Maritime Traffic.", "resolved_url": "https://www.wwf.org.hk/en/?26283/The-State-of-Hong-Kong-Biodiversity-2025#:~:text=In%20September%202020%2C%20WWF-Hong%20Kong%20helped%20create%20a%20working%20group%20to%20combat%20wildlife%20smuggling.%20The%20group%20was%20tasked%20with%20drafting%20and%20leading%20global%20consultations%20which%20led%20to%20the%202021%20passage%20of%20a%20new%20International%20Maritime%20Organization%20global%20document%2C%20Guidelines%20for%20the%20Prevention%20and%20Suppression%20of%20the%20Smuggling%20of%20Wildlife%20on%20Ships%20Engaged%20in%20International%20Maritime%20Traffic."}}, {"name": "Policy And Legal Advocacy", "source": {"source_type": "attached_report", "source_index": 0, "page_number": 15, "search_result_index": null, "quote": "Launched in January 2024, WWF-Hong Kong's Innovative Technology Solution for Detecting Undeclared and Mis-declared Wildlife Products in Cargo Bookings has now screened more than 7.3 million cargo bookings and identified over 25,000 high-risk shipments - a major advancement in combating IWT in maritime supply chains.", "resolved_url": "https://wwfhk.awsassets.panda.org/downloads/wwf_annual_review_2025_eng.pdf#page=15"}}, {"name": "Policy And Legal Advocacy", "source": {"source_type": "attached_report", "source_index": 0, "page_number": 15, "search_result_index": null, "quote": "WWF-Hong Kong supported two pioneering research initiatives to help authorities distinguish potentially illegal elephant ivory from legal mammoth ivory this year. In June 2025, the University of Hong Kong unveiled a breakthrough forensic method that uses a rapid, cost effective stable isotope analysis to identify ivory rapidly and cost effectively.", "resolved_url": "https://wwfhk.awsassets.panda.org/downloads/wwf_annual_review_2025_eng.pdf#page=15"}}, {"name": "Wildlife Conservation And Habitat Protection", "source": {"source_type": "attached_report", "source_index": 0, "page_number": 9, "search_result_index": null, "quote": "Reactivated in 2024, the Wetlands for Asian Flyways initiative (WAF) unites conservationists across Asia details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'Collectively, these efforts led to a 41% reduction (256,520 tonnes of CO2e) from business-as-usual scenarios, and a 48% reduction (348,180 tonnes of CO2e) compared to base year levels.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Impact data with beneficiary types is missing.

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: Organisation does not engage in direct animal sheltering; metric not applicable.

WWF-Hong Kong has been working since 1981 to deliver solutions for a living planet through conservation, footprint and education programmes, with the aim of transforming Hong Kong into Asia's most sustainable city.

metrics.context_qualifier: 16 of the top 20 global shipping companies, representing over 60% of the global shipping market, banned shark fin shipments.
metrics.metric_name: Global Shipping Companies Banning Shark Fin Shipments

source.quote: 16 of the top 20 global shipping companies, over 60% of the global shipping market, banned shark fin shipments.

metrics.context_qualifier: LOOP+ and LCMP efforts led to a 48% reduction (348,180 tonnes of CO2e) compared to base year levels.
metrics.metric_name: Carbon Emissions Reduction by LOOP+/LCMP (Base Year)

source.quote: Collectively, these efforts led to a 41% reduction (256,520 tonnes of CO2e) from business-as-usual scenarios, and a 48% reduction (348,180 tonnes of CO2e) compared to base year levels.

metrics.context_qualifier: LOOP+ and LCMP efforts led to a 41% reduction (256,520 tonnes of CO2e) from business-as-usual scenarios.

source.quote: Collectively, these efforts led to a 41% reduction (256,520 tonnes of CO2e) from business-as-usual scenarios, and a 48% reduction (348,180 tonnes of CO2e) compared to base year levels.

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative carbon emissions dropped by 321,540 tonnes of CO2e, a 44% reduction compared to base year measurements.

source.quote: Carbon emissions dropped by a cumulative 241,396 tonnes of CO2-equivalent, down 37% from business-as-usual scenarios (2022-23); and there was an aggregate reduction of 321,540 tonnes of CO2-equivalent - a 44% drop compared to base year measurements.

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative carbon emissions dropped by 241,396 tonnes of CO2e, a 37% reduction from business-as-usual scenarios (2022-23).

source.quote: Carbon emissions dropped by a cumulative 241,396 tonnes of CO2-equivalent, down 37% from business-as-usual scenarios (2022-23); and there was an aggregate reduction of 321,540 tonnes of CO2-equivalent - a 44% drop compared to base year measurements.

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 7.3 million cargo bookings screened for undeclared/mis-declared wildlife products.
metrics.metric_name: Cargo Bookings Screened for Wildlife Products

source.quote: Launched in January 2024, WWF-Hong Kong's Innovative Technology Solution for Detecting Undeclared and Mis-declared Wildlife Products in Cargo Bookings has now screened more than 7.3 million cargo bookings and identified over 25,000 high-risk shipments - a major advancement in combating IWT in maritime supply chains.

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 25,000 high-risk wildlife shipments identified from cargo bookings.

source.quote: Launched in January 2024, WWF-Hong Kong's Innovative Technology Solution for Detecting Undeclared and Mis-declared Wildlife Products in Cargo Bookings has now screened more than 7.3 million cargo bookings and identified over 25,000 high-risk shipments - a major advancement in combating IWT in maritime supply chains.

metrics.context_qualifier: Global population of Black-faced Spoonbill increased to over 2,700 birds from 288 in 1988-1990.

source.quote: The latest census has revealed a global population of over 2,700 birds, almost a ten-fold increase from a low of 288 individuals recorded in 1988-1990.

metrics.context_qualifier: IUCN status of Black-faced Spoonbill downlisted from 'Endangered' to 'Vulnerable' due to sustained conservation.

source.quote: The Black-faced spoonbill has benefited from sustained conservation actions through the years, with its IUCN status downlisted from "Endangered" to "Vulnerable."

metrics.context_qualifier: Asian Waterbird Conservation Fund backed 54 initiatives across 14 countries to conserve wetlands and migratory birds.

source.quote: The fund has backed 54 initiatives across 14 countries to date, boosting collective efforts to conserve vital wetlands and migratory bird populations along the East Asian-Australasian Flyway.

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 500 participants benefited from 30 leadership training sessions in wetland conservation.

source.quote: This year, more than 500 participants benefited from 30 leadership training sessions delivered by WWF-Hong Kong, reaffirming Mai Po's status as a regional wetland management hub.

metrics.context_qualifier: 6 companies adopted LOOP+ and 39 manufacturers participated in LCMP in FY25.

source.quote: In FY25, six companies adopted the LOOP+ and 39 manufacturers participated in the LCMP.

metrics.context_qualifier: 680 coral fragments nurtured in nursery and outplanted to Tolo Harbour and Channel, with 85% survival rate.

source.quote: So far, Reviving Our Corals has nurtured 680 coral fragments in the Hoi Ha Marine Life Centre nursery. These have been successfully outplanted to sites in the Tolo Harbour and Channel, and 85% have survived.

metrics.context_qualifier: 88 fish species observed at coral restoration sites, indicating marine life attraction.
metrics.metric_name: Fish Species Observed at Coral Restoration Sites

source.quote: The success of Reviving Our Corals is making a visible difference - the restored corals are attracting marine life, with 88 fish species having been observed at the sites.

significant_events.event_name: Global Shipping Companies Ban Shark Fin Shipments

source.quote: 16 of the top 20 global shipping companies, over 60% of the global shipping market, banned shark fin shipments.

significant_events.summary: Secured bans on shark fin shipments from 16 of the top 20 global shipping companies, representing over 60% of the market.

significant_events.event_name: Legislative Enhancements for Wildlife Trafficking

source.quote: It has also influenced legislative enhancements in 2021 to classify wildlife trafficking as organized crime, bolstering enforcement against smuggling.

significant_events.summary: Influenced 2021 legislative enhancements to classify wildlife trafficking as organized crime, bolstering enforcement.

significant_events.event_name: Contribution to Elephant Ivory Sales Prohibition

source.quote: Key achievements include advocating for a 2013 ban on trawling in Hong Kong waters to protect marine ecosystems, contributing to the 2021 prohibition on elephant ivory sales with severe penalties for violations, and supporting the creation of early marine parks such as Hoi Ha Wan in 1996.

significant_events.summary: Contributed to the 2021 prohibition on elephant ivory sales with severe penalties for violations.

significant_events.event_name: Advocacy for Trawling Ban in Hong Kong

source.quote: Key achievements include advocating for a 2013 ban on trawling in Hong Kong waters to protect marine ecosystems, contributing to the 2021 prohibition on elephant ivory sales with severe penalties for violations, and supporting the creation of early marine parks such as Hoi Ha Wan in 1996.

significant_events.summary: Advocated for and achieved a 2013 ban on trawling in Hong Kong waters to protect marine ecosystems.

significant_events.event_name: Creation of Working Group to Combat Wildlife Smuggling

source.quote: In September 2020, WWF-Hong Kong helped create a working group to combat wildlife smuggling. The group was tasked with drafting and leading global consultations which led to the 2021 passage of a new International Maritime Organization global document, Guidelines for the Prevention and Suppression of the Smuggling of Wildlife on Ships Engaged in International Maritime Traffic.

significant_events.summary: Helped create a working group in Sept 2020, leading to 2021 IMO guidelines for preventing wildlife smuggling on ships.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of Innovative Technology Solution for Wildlife Trade

source.quote: Launched in January 2024, WWF-Hong Kong's Innovative Technology Solution for Detecting Undeclared and Mis-declared Wildlife Products in Cargo Bookings has now screened more than 7.3 million cargo bookings and identified over 25,000 high-risk shipments - a major advancement in combating IWT in maritime supply chains.

significant_events.summary: Launched a tech solution in Jan 2024 to screen cargo bookings for undeclared/mis-declared wildlife products.

significant_events.event_name: Support for Forensic Research on Ivory Identification

source.quote: WWF-Hong Kong supported two pioneering research initiatives to help authorities distinguish potentially illegal elephant ivory from legal mammoth ivory this year. In June 2025, the University of Hong Kong unveiled a breakthrough forensic method that uses a rapid, cost effective stable isotope analysis to identify ivory rapidly and cost effectively.

significant_events.summary: Supported two research initiatives, including a new forensic method from HKU in June 2025, to distinguish illegal elephant ivory.

significant_events.event_name: Reactivation of Wetlands for Asian Flyways Initiative

source.quote: Reactivated in 2024, the Wetlands for Asian Flyways initiative (WAF) unites conservationists across Asia in work to protect wetland networks along the East Asian-Australasian and Central Asian Flyways.

significant_events.summary: Reactivated WAF initiative in 2024 to unite conservationists across Asia to protect wetland networks for migratory birds.

significant_events.event_name: International Collaboration Meeting for Black-faced Spoonbill

source.quote: In February 2025, conservation leaders from across the East Asian-Australasian Flyway (EAAF) convened at Mai Po to discuss strengthening international collaboration for the Black-faced spoonbill, a flagship species for conservation.

significant_events.summary: Convened conservation leaders in Feb 2025 at Mai Po to discuss strengthening collaboration for Black-faced spoonbill conservation.

significant_events.event_name: Release of The State of Hong Kong Biodiversity 2025 Report

source.quote: The 2025 fiscal year saw several landmark accomplishments - the release of The State of Hong Kong Biodiversity 2025 report, a collaboration with the Hong Kong Institute of Landscape Architects (HKILA) to promote Nature-based Solutions (NbS) in urban development, and community-led advocacy initiatives to preserve critical agricultural and wetland habitats.

significant_events.summary: Released a comprehensive report on Hong Kong's biodiversity, providing a scientific foundation for conservation policies.

significant_events.event_name: Collaboration with HKILA for Nature-based Solutions

source.quote: The 2025 fiscal year saw several landmark accomplishments - the release of The State of Hong Kong Biodiversity 2025 report, a collaboration with the Hong Kong Institute of Landscape Architects (HKILA) to promote Nature-based Solutions (NbS) in urban development, and community-led advocacy initiatives to preserve critical agricultural and wetland habitats.

significant_events.summary: Collaborated with HKILA to promote Nature-based Solutions (NbS) in urban development and infrastructure planning.

significant_events.event_name: Community-led Advocacy for Agricultural and Wetland Habitats

source.quote: The 2025 fiscal year saw several landmark accomplishments - the release of The State of Hong Kong Biodiversity 2025 report, a collaboration with the Hong Kong Institute of Landscape Architects (HKILA) to promote Nature-based Solutions (NbS) in urban development, and community-led advocacy initiatives to preserve critical agricultural and wetland habitats.

significant_events.summary: Led community advocacy initiatives to preserve critical agricultural and wetland habitats in Hong Kong.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of 'Growing Mai Po' Project

source.quote: Recognising the urgent need to preserve these irreplaceable natural and cultural landscapes, we launched the three-year Lee Hysan Foundation "Growing Mai Po" (GMP) project in June 2024, supported by the Lee Hysan Foundation.

significant_events.summary: Launched a three-year project in June 2024 to restore rice paddies as habitats for migratory birds in Mai Po.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of 'Smart Wetland 2.0' Project

source.quote: In July 2024, we launched the two-year "Smart Wetland 2.0" project, funded by the Countryside Conservation Fund Scheme under the Countryside Conservation Office.

significant_events.summary: Launched a two-year project in July 2024 to upgrade Mai Po's mammal monitoring system using smart technology.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of WWF Jockey Club Smart Wetland Management Project

source.quote: Thanks to a substantial grant from The Hong Kong Jockey Club Charities Trust, WWF-Hong Kong launched the WWF Jockey Club Smart Wetland Management Project ("The project") in 2025.

significant_events.summary: Launched a three-year project in 2025 to apply smart technology to wetland management in Mai Po.

significant_events.event_name: Reviving Our Corals Initiative

source.quote: So far, Reviving Our Corals has nurtured 680 coral fragments in the Hoi Ha Marine Life Centre nursery. These have been successfully outplanted to sites in the Tolo Harbour and Channel, and 85% have survived.

significant_events.summary: Nurtured and outplanted 680 coral fragments with 85% survival rate to restore marine ecosystems.

significant_events.event_name: Support for Marine Park Creation (Hoi Ha Wan)

source.quote: Key achievements include advocating for a 2013 ban on trawling in Hong Kong waters to protect marine ecosystems, contributing to the 2021 prohibition on elephant ivory sales with severe penalties for violations, and supporting the creation of early marine parks such as Hoi Ha Wan in 1996.

significant_events.summary: Supported the creation of early marine parks such as Hoi Ha Wan in 1996.

significant_events.event_name: Citizen Scientist Diver Training for Marine Litter Survey

source.quote: In the 2024 diving season, the project trained citizen scientist divers who led over 50 reef check teams to survey marine litter at Hong Kong's coral sites, providing valuable insights into local underwater conditions.

significant_events.summary: Trained citizen scientist divers who led over 50 reef check teams to survey marine litter at Hong Kong's coral sites in 2024.

significant_events.event_name: Mai Po Nature Reserve Receives Gold Star Wetland Centre Award

source.quote: On 27 July 2025, Mai Po Nature Reserve received the prestigious Gold Star Wetland Centre Award from Wetland Link International, joining just 15 other sites globally and becoming one of only three Gold Star centres worldwide.

significant_events.summary: Mai Po Nature Reserve received the prestigious Gold Star Wetland Centre Award from Wetland Link International in July 2025.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of East Asia's First Seafood Guide

source.quote: Launched East Asia's first “Seafood Guide” to promote sustainable seafood and raise awareness of marine biodiversity.

significant_events.summary: WWF-Hong Kong launched East Asia's first 'Seafood Guide' to promote sustainable seafood and marine biodiversity awareness.

significant_events.event_name: Earth Hour 2025

source.quote: As the world's largest grassroots environmental campaign, in 2025, Earth Hour once again highlighted Hong Kong's active role in climate action. On 22 March, nearly 4,000 companies, organisations and landmarks switched off non-essential lighting for an hour, demonstrating a collective commitment to reduce our carbon footprint and live sustainably.

significant_events.summary: Organized Earth Hour on 22 March 2025, with nearly 4,000 entities switching off lights to promote climate action.

significant_events.event_name: Walk for Nature 2025

source.quote: Walk for Nature 2025 took place on 22 and 23 November at Mai Po Nature Reserve, themed "Wetlands for Lives."

significant_events.summary: Held Walk for Nature on 22-23 Nov 2025 at Mai Po, engaging 1,300 participants in wetland habitat exploration.

significant_events.event_name: WWF-Hong Kong Flag Day 2025

source.quote: Held on 12 April, the WWF-Hong Kong Flag Day 2025 brought over 1,700 volunteers together to raise funds and awareness for our conservation work, underlining the vital role that community plays in our conservation successes.

significant_events.summary: Held Flag Day on 12 April 2025, engaging over 1,700 volunteers to raise funds and awareness for conservation.

significant_events.event_name: Establishment of Hong Kong's First 'Tranquility Path'

source.quote: We established Hong Kong's first "Tranquility Path" in May 2025, collaborating with the Mental Health Foundation (MHF) to create a space where stress can be relieved by being present in nature.

significant_events.summary: Established Hong Kong's first 'Tranquility Path' in May 2025 with MHF to promote mental wellness through nature.

data.original_text: From our work managing Mai Po Nature Reserve and conserving iconic species, we have expanded our mission to better serve Hong Kong's conservation and education needs.

WWF-Hong Kong has been working since 1981 to deliver solutions for a living planet through conservation, footprint and education programmes, with the aim of transforming Hong Kong into Asia's most sustainable city.

WWF-HK's core service is conservation. Environmental education is a tool to engage people in conservation and hence achieve our ultimate goal. WWF-HK's conservation programmes are divided into 5 pillars- Freshwater and Wetlands, Marine, Terrestrial and Conservation Policy, Oceans Sustainability and Climate Change.
Visitors are welcome at some five centres located Central 1, Tramway path, Island House Tai Po, Mai Po, and Hoi Ha Wan.
WWF-Hong Kong has been working since 1981 to deliver solutions for a living planet through Conservation, Footprint and Education programmes.
The past 40 years have seen us make many significant achievements in nature conservation, education and learning, and corporate and community engagement.
Launched East Asia's first “Seafood Guide” to promote sustainable seafood and raise awareness of marine biodiversity.
16 of the top 20 global shipping companies, over 60% of the global shipping market, banned shark fin shipments.
The Hong Kong Jockey Club Charities Trust awarded WWF-Hong Kong HK$347 million to transform Mai Po Nature Reserve into a 21st Century Nature Classroom.

The organization manages the Mai Po Nature Reserve, a Ramsar-designated wetland since 1995 that supports over 80,000 migratory birds and more than 2,050 recorded species, in partnership with Hong Kong's Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department since 1983.

Key achievements include advocating for a 2013 ban on trawling in Hong Kong waters to protect marine ecosystems, contributing to the 2021 prohibition on elephant ivory sales with severe penalties for violations, and supporting the creation of early marine parks such as Hoi Ha Wan in 1996.
It has also influenced legislative enhancements in 2021 to classify wildlife trafficking as organized crime, bolstering enforcement against smuggling.
WWF-Hong Kong and the Mai Po Management Committee are honored to be the first-ever Hong Kong conservation organization to receive the BirdLife International Conservation Achievement Award.

The award recognizes WWF and other conservation organizations' efforts and achievements in protecting the endangered Black-faced spoonbill. The latest census has revealed a global population of over 2,700 birds, almost a ten-fold increase from a low of 288 individuals recorded in 1988-1990.

This year, we continued our wetland conservation work by maintaining and enhancing the Mai Po wetlands to ensure they remain a biodiversity-rich habitat for wildlife and a suitable wintering ground for migratory birds.

We also worked to empower managers of other sites along the East Asian- Australasian Flyway, building regional climate resilience and helping these wetlands remain vital, vibrant places where migratory birds and other wildlife can thrive.

The 2025 fiscal year saw several landmark accomplishments – the release of The State of Hong Kong Biodiversity 2025 report, a collaboration with the Hong Kong Institute of Landscape Architects (HKILA) to promote Nature-based Solutions (NbS) in urban development, and community-led advocacy initiatives to preserve critical agricultural and wetland habitats.

We marked the first anniversary of completing the Mai Po Peter Scott Infrastructure Upgrade Project. The new Peter Scott Visitor Centre has emerged as a hub for wetland conservation, research, education, and community engagement.

In 2018, WWF-Hong Kong began establishing a strong conservation foundation at Shui Hau on Lantau Island, employing a combination of ecological surveys, public education and active community engagement. This year, we launched two initiatives, “Discover Shui Hau” and “Smart Shui Hau”, to aid in the development of a holistic management plan for the area by boosting community involvement, education and visitor management.

In February 2024, we released Adopting Nature-based Solutions for a Better Hong Kong as a visionary study, hoping to raise local awareness about NbS and explore its applications in Hong Kong and the Greater Bay Area (GBA).

The report is accompanied by the Hong Kong Terrestrial Biodiversity Hotspot Map 2025, which highlights 27 critical biodiversity hotspots outside the protected-area system. These hotspots, which cover merely 6% of Hong Kong's land area, are home to about 95% of at-risk species.

In September 2020, WWF-Hong Kong helped create a working group to combat wildlife smuggling. The group was tasked with drafting and leading global consultations which led to the 2021 passage of a new International Maritime Organization global document, Guidelines for the Prevention and Suppression of the Smuggling of Wildlife on Ships Engaged in International Maritime Traffic.

aliases: World Wide Fund for Nature Hong Kong
